answer:
Bootstrapping is preferred when we want the data’s distribution to match actual data without assuming a known parametric form, especially when the actual distribution is unknown or the sample is small.  
Thus, (i) is correct because bootstrapping resamples from actual data to preserve its distribution, (iii) is correct because bootstrapping does not require knowing the distribution, and (iv) is correct because bootstrapping can still provide inference from a small sample.  
(ii) is incorrect because pure simulation is better when the distribution is known exactly.

\boxed{H}
